key_insight: Detonation spraying can build strong Ti/HAp functionally graded coatings on Ti-6Al-4V, with a smoother gradient improving adhesion.
engineering_relevance: These graded bioceramic coatings are promising for load-bearing implant surfaces because they combine a titanium-rich bond layer with a hydroxyapatite-rich bioactive top layer.
method: Two Ti/HAp gradient coating architectures were deposited by detonation spraying.
SEM and EDS were used to verify layer structure and elemental gradients.
Raman spectroscopy confirmed hydroxyapatite phase retention.
evidence_signal: Adhesion testing (ASTM C633-13) showed 45.78 ± 4.4 MPa for Configuration 1 and 52.32 ± 6.7 MPa for Configuration 2, both well above the 15 MPa minimum.
watch_item: Results are promising, but long-term in-service durability and performance under cyclic biological/mechanical loading still need validation.
paper_title: Detonation Spraying of Functionally Graded Hydroxyapatite/Titanium Coatings on Ti–6Al–4V Alloy
